name: Formaldehyde solution ~37% (formalin) / buffered formalin
_disclaimer: FIELD QUICK-REFERENCE ONLY. This is NOT the authoritative Safety Data Sheet. Always consult the manufacturer's current SDS for the exact product and concentration. In an emergency call your local poison control / emergency number.
hazards: Toxic if swallowed/inhaled/in contact with skin (H301/H311/H331)
Causes severe skin burns and eye damage (H314)
May cause an allergic skin reaction (H317)
Suspected of causing cancer (H351) / carcinogen per IARC

first_aid.eyes: Rinse immediately with water 15+ min, hold lids open, get medical attention urgently.
first_aid.skin: Remove contaminated clothing, wash with plenty of water/soap, seek medical attention for burns.
first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately; get medical attention if coughing/breathing difficulty.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth, drink water if conscious, call poison control immediately.

fire: Non-/low-flammable as dilute solution but concentrated formaldehyde vapor is combustible. Use water spray, foam, CO2, dry chemical. Toxic fumes in fire.
spill: Evacuate/ventilate, wear full PPE, absorb with inert material, neutralize per institutional protocol, do NOT flush to drains.
storage: Cool, well-ventilated, tightly closed, away from oxidizers and incompatible materials; segregate from acids/bases.
field_notes: Serious inhalation and skin hazard — prefer pre-buffered dilute formalin, work upwind/ventilated, and minimize open transfers. Consider ethanol or propylene glycol where the study allows.
